data_stores.name: Woodpecker pipeline history
data_stores.notes: Pipeline logs and repository activation metadata can contain branch names, repository details, and operator-trigger context, so they remain private and restorable with the Woodpecker control plane.

data_stores.name: Browser runner bounded session artifacts
data_stores.notes: Captured screenshots, PDFs, selector text, and downloaded files may contain third-party page content or delegated-session data, so retention stays intentionally short and private.

data_stores.name: Changedetection watch history and diff datastore
data_stores.notes: Changedetection stores repo-managed watch metadata, snapshots, diff history, and the most recent drift-free sync report for upstream content monitoring.

data_stores.name: Crawl4AI transient crawl buffers
locations: Transient request and response buffers inside the crawl4ai container on docker-runtime
Ephemeral browser session and temporary extraction state inside the Crawl4AI runtime on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Crawl4AI is stateless in this deployment and does not persist crawl history after each synchronous Markdown or extraction response completes.

data_stores.name: Directus operational metadata database
locations: Directus system tables plus governed collections in the public schema of the dedicated directus database
data_stores.notes: Directus persists repo-governed operational metadata in a dedicated PostgreSQL database. Upstream Directus 11.17.0 keeps both system tables and managed collections in the public schema of that dedicated database, so schema isolation is enforced at the database boundary rather than by a separate PostgreSQL schema.

data_stores.name: Dozzle live log stream cache
locations: Docker stdout and stderr log buffers on docker-runtime
Docker stdout and stderr log buffers on docker-build
Docker stdout and stderr log buffers on monitoring
data_stores.notes: Dozzle is read-only and does not persist its own datastore; it exposes recent container log buffers from the managed Docker hosts for real-time debugging.

data_stores.name: Drift detection receipts
data_stores.notes: Drift findings are operationally useful across recent investigations but are superseded by longer-term audit evidence.

data_stores.name: Generated Excalidraw scene set
data_stores.notes: The committed architecture scene sources are regenerated from repo truth and remain part of normal repository backup scope.

data_stores.name: Flagsmith project, environment, and feature state
data_stores.notes: Flagsmith persists organisations, projects, environments, feature definitions, segment metadata, and environment-specific flag state in the shared PostgreSQL cluster.

data_stores.name: Ephemeral fixture receipts
data_stores.notes: Fixture receipts track temporary environments and can be discarded after the short-lived debugging window closes.

data_stores.name: Validation gate bypass receipts
data_stores.notes: Bypass records capture exception handling and should remain available for annual audit review.

data_stores.name: Gitea repositories and metadata
data_stores.notes: Repositories, issues, webhook configuration, and audit metadata are operational records that should remain restorable for multi-year incident and change reconstruction.

data_stores.name: Gotenberg transient render buffers
locations: Transient request and response buffers inside the gotenberg container on docker-runtime
Ephemeral temporary files created during Chromium and LibreOffice conversions on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Gotenberg is stateless and does not persist source documents or rendered PDFs after each synchronous conversion finishes.

data_stores.name: GlitchTip grouped issues, events, and DSN project artifacts
data_stores.notes: GlitchTip stores grouped exception payloads, issue metadata, notification state, uploaded artifacts, and the repo-managed DSN project catalogue for first-party producers.

data_stores.name: Tesseract OCR transient request buffers
locations: Transient uploaded image and PDF buffers inside the tesseract-ocr container on docker-runtime
Ephemeral rendered page images created during PDF OCR on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: The OCR runtime is stateless and discards uploaded source files, rendered page images, and extracted text after each synchronous request completes.

data_stores.name: Grafana annotations
data_stores.notes: Annotations capture deployment and incident context that remains useful longer than raw logs.

data_stores.name: Harbor registry artifacts and chart storage
data_stores.notes: Private container images, OCI artifacts, and retained charts are durable release assets that must remain recoverable through the managed retention window.

data_stores.name: Harbor registry metadata and scan results
data_stores.notes: Harbor stores project metadata, robot-account policy, replication state, and vulnerability scan findings that support supply-chain auditing and recovery.

data_stores.name: Homepage generated dashboard config
data_stores.notes: Homepage stores only repo-generated dashboard configuration and lightweight runtime state for the unified service dashboard.

data_stores.name: Image scan receipts
data_stores.notes: Security scan evidence should remain available across one full patching and review cycle.

data_stores.name: Redpanda durable log store
data_stores.notes: Topic retention remains per-topic and repo-managed; the named Docker volume must be covered by docker-runtime backup evidence whenever the Redpanda runtime contract changes.

data_stores.name: Langfuse event and media object storage
data_stores.notes: Blob-backed event uploads and media attachments may contain raw prompts, responses, and attached evidence artifacts.

data_stores.name: Langfuse trace and observation store
data_stores.notes: Langfuse captures prompt, trace, and generation payloads that may contain operator-entered context and model outputs.

data_stores.name: Live-apply receipts
data_stores.notes: Deployment evidence remains part of the short-term operational timeline and is summarized into longer-lived release history.

data_stores.name: Loki log store
data_stores.notes: Operational logs may contain internal IP addresses, usernames, and error details.

data_stores.name: Mailpit captured message cache
locations: Mailpit in-memory message store on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Mailpit intentionally keeps intercepted staging and development messages in ephemeral in-memory storage so assertion traffic is inspectable but lost on restart.

data_stores.name: Piper voice model cache
data_stores.notes: Piper stores downloaded voice models in a named Docker volume so the private TTS runtime can restart without re-fetching the declared model set.

data_stores.name: Piper transient synthesis payloads
locations: Transient request and response buffers inside the piper container on docker-runtime
Ephemeral synthesized WAV bytes returned synchronously to callers on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Piper does not persist input text or synthesized audio after each request completes, but both may contain sensitive operator or agent content while the request is in flight.

data_stores.name: Matrix Synapse events and room state
data_stores.notes: Conversation history, room membership, and server-to-user metadata are retained for two years for assistant auditability and recovery.

data_stores.name: Matrix Synapse media store
data_stores.notes: Uploaded Matrix media is retained for one year unless a stricter room- or user-level policy is introduced later.

data_stores.name: Mattermost messages and attachments
data_stores.notes: ChatOps history supports incident reconstruction and should retain two years of operator discussion.

data_stores.name: Mutation audit log
data_stores.notes: Audit events record operator and automation identities and must remain available for at least one annual review cycle.

data_stores.name: NetBox object change log
data_stores.notes: Object-change history should remain available through at least one quarterly planning cycle without growing unbounded.

data_stores.name: Paperless document archive
locations: Docker volumes paperless-media, paperless-data, paperless-export, and paperless-consume on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Paperless stores uploaded source files, OCR output, and exportable archive artifacts that must remain recoverable.

data_stores.name: Paperless document metadata
data_stores.notes: Paperless stores document metadata, taxonomy, correspondents, and operator actions alongside the archive state.

data_stores.name: Nextcloud account and sharing metadata
data_stores.notes: Account records, shares, app state, and file metadata define the canonical personal data plane and must remain restorable with the runtime filesystem.

data_stores.name: Nextcloud personal files and DAV payloads
data_stores.notes: User-owned files, attachments, calendar bodies, contacts, and task payloads remain under user-directed retention and must stay recoverable.

data_stores.name: OpenBao KV secrets
data_stores.notes: Secret material is retained until explicit deletion or rotation removes the prior value.

data_stores.name: OpenFGA authorization graph
locations: serverclaw-authz store, authorization models, and tuples
data_stores.notes: The delegated authorization graph persists named operator and service-principal relationships until the repo-managed bootstrap contract removes or replaces them.

data_stores.name: Grist document and instance store
data_stores.notes: Grist persists operator-managed spreadsheet documents and instance metadata as SQLite files under /persist on docker-runtime.

data_stores.name: Temporal workflow history and visibility
data_stores.notes: Temporal persists workflow event history, timers, and visibility records for the repo-managed `lv3` namespace using the declared retention window.

data_stores.name: ServerClaw workbench state
data_stores.notes: ServerClaw persists conversations, workspace artifacts, prompt history, and user-specific workbench settings in its local runtime data volume on docker-runtime.

data_stores.name: Outline attachment storage
data_stores.notes: S3-compatible MinIO storage holds attachment objects and future uploaded evidence referenced from wiki documents.

data_stores.name: Outline knowledge metadata
data_stores.notes: Outline stores workspace, user, collection, and document metadata that may include operator identities and operational text.

data_stores.name: Lago billing catalog and usage state
data_stores.notes: Lago stores billable metrics, plans, customers, subscriptions, invoices, credit notes, and aggregated usage records that may include tenant identifiers and billing contacts.

data_stores.name: Lago generated billing artifacts
data_stores.notes: The Lago storage directory retains generated invoice exports, PDF-ready billing artifacts, and other runtime files needed for billing-history reconstruction.

data_stores.name: Plane attachment object storage
data_stores.notes: Plane attachment uploads and exported artifacts are persisted in the local MinIO-backed object store on docker-runtime.

data_stores.name: Plane workspace and issue store
data_stores.notes: Plane stores project boards, issue metadata, and operator-entered planning content that should remain recoverable across normal backup cycles.

data_stores.name: Plausible analytics event store
data_stores.notes: ClickHouse stores privacy-preserving pageview and referral aggregates for the declared public LV3 sites without raw IP retention.

data_stores.name: Plausible site, user, and configuration metadata
data_stores.notes: PostgreSQL-backed site configuration, dashboard state, and the bootstrap recovery user metadata remain restorable with the runtime volumes.

data_stores.name: Shared object storage buckets and IAM metadata
data_stores.notes: Repo-managed MinIO stores shared service objects, bucket metadata, and bucket-level lifecycle policy state for Loki, Langfuse exports, Gitea LFS, and RAG staging.

data_stores.name: Platform-context staging object storage
data_stores.notes: Raw document uploads stage in the shared MinIO bucket before extraction and should expire quickly to limit retained sensitive source material.

data_stores.name: Promotion receipts
data_stores.notes: Promotion evidence remains useful for release forensics for one year.

data_stores.name: Semaphore task history
data_stores.notes: Task output can contain operator context, playbook names, and limited execution logs, so it should stay private and be retained long enough for incident review.

data_stores.name: ServerClaw workspace and chat state
data_stores.notes: ServerClaw persists chat history, workspace metadata, uploaded knowledge artifacts, and user-facing assistant settings in its ServerClaw data volume on coolify.

data_stores.name: Tempo trace blocks
data_stores.notes: Trace payloads support short-lived troubleshooting and should remain bounded to protect monitoring disk capacity.

data_stores.name: Apache Tika transient document extraction payloads
locations: Transient request and response buffers inside the tika container on docker-runtime
Ephemeral extracted plaintext and metadata returned synchronously to callers on docker-runtime
data_stores.notes: Apache Tika is stateless and does not persist source documents, extracted text, or metadata after each synchronous request completes, but uploaded documents may contain sensitive content in transit.

data_stores.name: TLS private keys
data_stores.notes: Runtime keys are rotated with certificate renewal and old material should not persist beyond the overlap window.

data_stores.name: Typesense platform service index
data_stores.notes: The repo-managed platform service catalog is materialized into Typesense as a derived full-text and faceted-search index and can be replayed from git after restore.

data_stores.name: Label Studio annotation metadata database
data_stores.notes: Label Studio stores project metadata, task assignments, task payload references, and completed annotations in the dedicated PostgreSQL database.

data_stores.name: Label Studio local runtime state
data_stores.notes: The Label Studio data directory stores local imports, exports, and attachment cache material that accompanies the repo-managed project catalog.

data_stores.name: Superset metadata and managed BI assets
locations: PostgreSQL database postgres on postgres, schema superset
data_stores.notes: Superset persists datasource definitions, saved queries, charts, dashboards, and user-generated BI metadata in the managed superset schema, so the schema should remain recoverable with the shared PostgreSQL backup policy.

data_stores.name: Vaultwarden shared credential vault
data_stores.notes: Shared operator credentials, recovery codes, and TOTP seeds remain until explicit deletion or rotation removes them from the private vault.

data_stores.name: Windmill job history
data_stores.notes: Workflow logs can contain operator context and transient execution metadata but do not need indefinite retention.
